weaknesses.description: Default archetype has no structured playbook. The classifier maps Default to methodology-damodaran.md, a methodology overview, not a playbook with the uniform skeleton (identify, why breaks, drivers table, engine mapping, input deltas, bear checklist, master lens, self-check, traps) that all four specialist archetype playbooks share. Most companies fall into Default; the edge cases get better structure.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Write playbook-default.md following the uniform 9-section skeleton: (1) Identify plus mis-classification trap distinguishing default from specialist archetypes. (2) Why the default FCFF frame works for operating companies with durable margins. (3) Right drivers table with default readings. (4) Engine mapping to dcf_valuation.py with worked template. (5) Input-estimation deltas vs specialist archetypes. (6) Bear checklist with leading indicators for moat erosion and disruption. (7) Master lens: Damodaran explicitly named as default anchor. (8) Self-check gate. (9) Common traps (extrapolating growth, understating fade, ignoring terminal dominance).

weaknesses.location: playbook-financials.md L113, report-voice.md L85, self-audit-gate.md L65
weaknesses.description: Monte Carlo gap for financials. playbook-financials.md states monte_carlo is for FCFF archetypes and financials should build MoS bands by stressing ROE and credit cost, but provides no concrete methodology, script, or worked example. report-voice.md and self-audit-gate.md require a margin-of-safety buy-band for every report. A financials report cannot satisfy this requirement with documented tools.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either add a financial-specific stress script (stress_financials.py) that produces a percentile band by varying ROE, credit cost, and Ke; or document a deterministic process for building the financials MoS band with a worked example in financials.example.json. Update report-voice.md to note the archetype-specific MoS mechanism.

weaknesses.description: Loop protocol self-documents as incomplete. The compliance table shows 3 of 5 same-family-safe conditions as NOT met: Type-B gates are currently self-judged with no cross-model routing, adversarial reviews are not saved as artifacts, and the adversarial reviewer is same model family. The elaborate cross-model verdict routing and artifact system described in sections 2 and 3 is aspirational infrastructure that does not yet exist.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either build the cross-model routing infrastructure (Codex integration for Type-B gates, verdict artifact persistence) or downgrade the protocol to describe only what is operational. Add an Implementation Status section at the top marking each feature as OPERATIONAL or PLANNED.

weaknesses.description: Archetype count and playbook structure mismatch. The classifier defines six archetypes (Default, Financials, Cyclical, Young, Mature, Holding-co), but only four have playbooks following the uniform skeleton. holding-company-sotp.md covers SOTP methodology but lacks the playbook skeleton: no mis-classification trap, no bear checklist with leading indicators, no self-check gate, no common traps section. Default also lacks a playbook. 2 of 6 archetypes have weaker reference coverage.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Elevate holding-company-sotp.md to a full playbook-holding-co.md following the uniform skeleton. Add: bear checklist (conglomerate discount factors, regulatory risk, capital-allocation agency problem), master lens, self-check gate, common traps (double-counting cash, ignoring VIE risk, treating all stakes at stated value).

weaknesses.description: Terminal value percentage documentation is inconsistent. methodology-damodaran.md mentions NVIDIA terminal at 77 percent with a generic warning (above 75 percent, flag it). playbook-young.md documents terminal at 108 percent as the young-company signature. playbook-mature.md documents terminal at 47 percent as the mature signature. There is no unified terminal-value diagnostic table showing what percentage means per archetype. An analyst using only methodology-damodaran.md would flag a young company as problematic when 108 percent terminal share is actually the correct diagnostic signature.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Add a unified terminal-value diagnostic table to either methodology-damodaran.md or input-estimation.md: Young (terminal above 100 percent, negative explicit FCFF, survival-weighted), Default/Growth (terminal 60 to 80 percent, heavy reliance on terminal assumptions), Mature (terminal below 50 percent, near-term cash dominates), Cyclical (terminal roughly 60 percent, terminal ROC near CoC). Each with whether the percentage is a warning or a signature.

weaknesses.description: Default archetype methodology is fragmented across 4 documents with no single entry point. An analyst must read methodology-damodaran.md (6-layer structure, 4 drivers), valuation-lenses.md (4 lenses), input-estimation.md (driver estimation), and analyst-playbook.md (analyst techniques, 10-step checklist) to get what a specialist archetype gets from 1 playbook. The Default archetype is the most common; this fragmentation is a usability problem.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Creating playbook-default.md (GAP-001) would resolve most of this by providing a single entry point that references the other docs as depth resources rather than requiring them as pre-reading.

weaknesses.description: No turnaround or distressed archetype. Both playbook-young.md (references turnaround handling) and playbook-mature.md (references distressed firms in Damodaran dark side) acknowledge this as a real category but neither provides methodology. Distressed valuation has genuinely different mechanics: enterprise value from asset sales, debt trading levels, recovery waterfalls, probability of reorganization vs liquidation.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Consider a playbook-distressed.md covering distress classification (liquidity vs solvency crisis), recovery analysis by asset class and debt tranche, debt-market-implied probabilities, and the Damodaran distressed-firm framework. Alternatively, explicitly document that distressed firms are out of scope.

weaknesses.description: Financials playbook lumps fundamentally different business models. Life insurers (embedded value methodology), P&C insurers (float analysis with combined ratio), deposit banks (NIM and credit cycle), and mortgage REITs (spread duration and prepayment risk) have genuinely different valuation mechanics. The playbook covers each briefly in section 5 but depth is uneven: banks get most coverage, insurers get one paragraph, REITs and broker-dealers are only mentioned in the classifier.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either expand section 5 with dedicated subsections for each sub-type (Banks, P&C Insurers, Life Insurers, Specialty Lenders/BDCs, Mortgage REITs) with worked input examples, or split into separate bank and insurance playbooks if depth warrants.

weaknesses.description: Duplication and drift risk between methodology-damodaran.md and playbook-young.md on the dark side of valuation. playbook-young.md section 7 re-explains the failure-branch, 3P discipline, and narrative discipline concepts already established in methodology-damodaran.md. If Damodaran updates his framework, two documents need updating. Future edits could cause the two docs to drift apart with contradictory formulations.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: In playbook-young.md section 7, reference methodology-damodaran.md as the canonical Damodaran framework and only add young-specific operationalization (how to set failure_p, how cash runway maps to survival). Do not re-explain concepts the methodology doc already owns.

weaknesses.description: No pharma or biotech pipeline playbook. input-estimation.md mentions pharma for R&D useful life. playbook-young.md mentions clinical-stage biotech as a sub-case. But biotech has unique valuation mechanics not covered: rNPV by trial phase, patent-cliff modeling, regulatory binary-event probability-weighting, peak-sales estimation. The young playbook uses a single failure_p which does not capture the staged derisking of a drug pipeline across Phase 1, 2, 3, and regulatory approval.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either extend playbook-young.md with a pharma-specific subsection covering rNPV by phase, patent-cliff modeling, and staged-probability approach; or explicitly document that biotech pipeline valuation requires external domain-specific tools and note the gap.

weaknesses.description: report-voice.md references ../templates/report.example.md as a worked example in the correct register, but no such file exists. The templates directory contains only assumption JSON files and schema files, no full worked report. The voice guide asks analysts to match a benchmark they cannot study.
weaknesses.suggestedFix: Either create templates/report.example.md (a full Mode A report for NVIDIA or Tencent, 3500 to 5000 words, demonstrating the investor-facing register) or remove the reference and replace with inline short excerpts from a real Damodaran post that demonstrate the voice.

strengths: Six-archetype classifier with clear decision tree (classify-archetype/SKILL.md) provides unambiguous routing to correct engine and playbook
Four specialized playbooks follow a consistent 9-section skeleton enabling an analyst to move between archetypes without relearning the structure

analyst-playbook.md synthesizes 7 distinct analyst styles (sell-side, SemiAnalysis supply-chain, capex bridge, Damodaran DCF, Mauboussin reverse DCF, bear case, compounder) with reusable company-agnostic techniques
valuation-lenses.md provides a strong triangulation framework: interpret the pattern of agreement/disagreement between lenses rather than averaging them - a genuine analytical insight
live-tracking.md currency layer with driver-delta discipline (CONFIRMS, STRENGTHENS, BREAKS) is sophisticated and directly enables Mode C refresh longevity
input-estimation.md is comprehensive: covers all four drivers plus terminal value with a concrete estimation checklist and accounting adjustments
holding-company-sotp.md covers the stub/implied-core cross-check and value-trap distinction cleanly with a worked Tencent example
report-voice.md anti-examples table provides concrete before/after fixes that make the investor-facing register operational, not abstract
self-audit-gate.md reuses the Mode B critique rubric (7 dimensions) turned inward, avoiding parallel standard drift
loop-protocol.md gate taxonomy (Type-A machine-checkable vs Type-B taste/judgment) with convergence terminator provides a real safety mechanism against infinite loops
report-critique-rubric.md severity levels (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) with a concrete verdict rule is rigorous and mirrors code-review practice
All 10 referenced engine scripts exist in the scripts/ directory (dcf_valuation.py through report_lint.py), and all 5 archetype templates exist in templates/
Playbook cross-references are accurate: each playbook references the correct engine, template, and SKILL.md classifier
